description: The searchParams-flattening helper (Record<string,string|string[]|undefined> -> Record<string,string>) is copy-pasted into 18 page modules under the names `flat` and `flatten`. The copies have drifted into three behaviourally distinct variants: (a) the guarded form shown here (`v.length > 0 && v[0] !== undefined`), (b) a truthy form `if (v[0]) out[k] = v[0]` (app/me/market/page.tsx:325, app/chestshop/items/[itemKey]/page.tsx:307) which drops legitimately-empty-string array params, and (c) a strings-only form `if (typeof v === 'string') out[k] = v` (app/admin/accounts/page.tsx:78) which silently discards array-valued params entirely. Divergent behaviour across copies of the same helper is exactly the DRY hazard: a query-string edge case behaves differently depending on which page received it.
recommendation: Extract one shared helper (e.g. `flattenSearchParams` in lib/, colocated with the zod SP parsing) with a single agreed semantics for array-valued and empty-string params, and replace all 18 local definitions with an import. Pick the guarded variant (a) as canonical.

if (Array.isArray(v)) { if (v[0]) out[k] = v[0]; } else if (v !== undefined) out[k] = v;
if (Array.isArray(v)) { if (v[0]) out[k] = v[0]; } else if (v !== undefined) out[k] = v;

verification_note: Snippet at app/transactions/page.tsx:181-189 matches verbatim. Confirmed 18 copies of the helper across app/. Variant (b) truthy form 'if (v[0]) out[k] = v[0]' confirmed at app/me/market/page.tsx:328 (~325) and app/chestshop/items/[itemKey]/page.tsx:310 (~307), which drops empty-string first elements. Variant (c) strings-only 'if (typeof v === "string")' confirmed at app/admin/accounts/page.tsx:78, which discards array-valued params. The three behaviorally-distinct copies are real and the DRY/drift hazard holds. Minor is fair: it is duplicated-helper drift with query-param edge-case differences, not a correctness bug under normal (non-empty string) params. (Note: only the name 'flat' was observed, not 'flatten', but that detail doesn't affect the substance.)

description: Two `as never` casts on a write path suppress Kysely's Insertable/Updateable column-name and type checking for the explorer_identity insert/upsert. The explorer_identity table is fully typed in the DB registry (lib/db.ts:41-47) and uuidToBin returns Buffer matching player_uuid_bin, so the cast is not required by an untyped registry entry — it blanket-erases the value-object type, meaning a future typo in a column name or a wrong-typed value on this economy-identity write would compile silently rather than being caught. This is the `as`-cast-hiding-type-errors anti-pattern the charter calls out, on a mutation path.
recommendation: Remove the `as never` casts and let the value objects type-check against the registry. If Kysely rejects them because a generated/default column (linked_at) is declared required, mark that column via Kysely's Generated<> in the DB interface (or ColumnType) rather than casting the whole payload to never.
